themes.hook: Your boss isn't asking you to work from home because of 'culture'—it’s because of the Strait of Hormuz.
key_data_points: Delhi commercial LPG supply capped at 20% of average consumption
8-10% projected hit to City Gas Distribution (CGD) volumes due to West Asia conflict
Major IT firms like Infosys and HCLTech shifting to hybrid work specifically due to fuel shortages
themes.non_obvious_angle: We usually view energy security as a macro-economic or industrial problem. In reality, India's energy vulnerability is now directly dictating urban labor patterns and corporate operational mandates.
themes.your_take_prompt: What's your stance on how Indian companies should hedge against 'energy-induced' operational risks beyond just basic business continuity planning?

source_articles.title: Infosys, HCLTech advise employees to bring food, shift to hybrid work amid commercial LPG shortage
Delhi government caps commercial LPG supply at 20%
Middle East disruptions to impact daily CGD sales volume by 8-10%

themes.hook: HDFC Bank is trading at its cheapest valuation in 20 years (1.7x FY28e), yet the 'smart money' is rotating into PSU banks. The private bank alpha is dying.
key_data_points: HDFC Bank P/B ratio at 1.7x FY28e—a multi-decade low
NBFC-MSME credit gap remains a massive structural bottleneck despite high liquidity
PSU banks are currently outperforming private peers in credit growth segments
themes.non_obvious_angle: The 'Private is better' thesis was built on superior technology and retail credit expansion. Now that technology is a commodity (UPI/AA) and retail credit is saturated, the valuation moat is evaporating as PSUs clean their books.
themes.your_take_prompt: Is the current derating of private banks a value trap or a generational entry point for the next decade?

source_articles.title: Case for P/B compression for private banks? Sridhar Sivaram of Enam thinks so
India’s Credit Paradox – NBFCs & MSME Credit Gap

themes.hook: The Indian government just gave a 'VIP pass' to mega-conglomerates. The new IPO rules are designed to prevent a market indigestion that would crash the index.
key_data_points: Finance Ministry amended public float rules for large IPO-bound firms
Lowered stake dilution requirements for mega-listings
Jio IPO timeline is being synced with these specific regulatory tweaks
themes.non_obvious_angle: This isn't just 'Ease of Doing Business.' It's a tactical move to allow massive entities like Jio to list without forcing a ₹50,000+ crore liquidity drain on the secondary market in one go.
themes.your_take_prompt: Does lowering the public float requirement for large firms compromise minority shareholder protection in the long run?

source_articles.title: Big news for IPO-bound firms: Finance Ministry amends public float rules
SEBI CHAIRMAN Says Govt has notified the rules for large IPOs

themes.hook: Stop obsessing over EV sales and ₹5 lakh discounts. The real war for India’s green transition is being fought in Nagpur and Delhi's recycling centers.
key_data_points: Waaree Energies 10 GW integrated ingot and wafer facility—India's largest
Delhi EV Policy 2.0 introduces a dedicated battery recycling framework
₹5 lakh discounts on EVs hit in March to meet PLI and fiscal targets
themes.non_obvious_angle: We are moving from the 'Incentive Era' (FAME/Discounts) to the 'Industrial Era' of EVs. The winners won't be those who sell the most cars, but those who control the upstream (wafers) and the downstream (recycling).
themes.your_take_prompt: Will India's focus on indigenizing the 'back-end' of the EV chain succeed, or will we remain dependent on Chinese raw materials for another decade?

source_articles.title: Waaree Energies Announces India's Largest 10 GW Integrated Ingot and Wafer Manufacturing Facility
Delhi EV policy 2.0 to bring dedicated battery recycling framework
EV discounts hit ₹5 lakh as fuel jitters and PLI targets drive sales

themes.hook: India's new labor codes aren't just about 'formalization'—they are the blueprints for a nation of perpetual gig workers.
key_data_points: New labor codes driving wider adoption of 'fixed-term' employment
Rise of 'Instant House-help Startups' formalizing domestic labor via venture capital
Supreme Court rejection of nationwide menstrual leave citing career risks for women
themes.non_obvious_angle: We are witnessing the 'Uber-ization' of the Indian household. By formalizing domestic help and encouraging fixed-term contracts, we aren't creating stable jobs; we are commoditizing labor to maximize corporate and middle-class convenience.
themes.your_take_prompt: Is the shift toward 'flexi-staffing' a necessary evil for India's growth, or are we building an economic house of cards?

source_articles.title: New labour codes to drive wider adoption of fixed-term employment
The rise of India's 'Instant House-help Startups'
Supreme Court rejects plea for nationwide menstrual leave policy
